description: Ambiguous requests that the implicit catalog router must resolve without loading all leaf skills.

cases.prompt: Our MVP serves three audiences and has six primary actions. Help us decide what it is.
This AI settings screen works, but people say it feels cold and intimidating.
The redesign is dramatically different, but we cannot explain why it is better.
Our launch page has nine feature claims and no memorable promise.
The keynote is tomorrow and our live demo fails unpredictably after the second transition.
We say our model is 37 percent faster, but the audience does not know whether that matters.
We need a high-bar hiring decision based on a candidate's work, not pedigree or charisma.
Seven people can block this roadmap decision and nobody owns the deadline.
Bad news gets softened before it reaches the founder and dissenters stop speaking up.
We have four products, nine months of runway, and cannot fund all of them.
The first business failed, but one internal capability has surprising customer pull.
Everyone says procurement makes this impossible, but nobody has checked the actual rule.
Turn my complete book notes into a few reusable practices with provenance.
This ambitious plan may work, but it is already damaging sleep, health, and relationships.
Shipments are up sharply, but partner inventory and end-customer activation are unclear.
I want to enter this market mainly because a former partner said we could not.
We raised far more than planned and now every team wants to scale before retention is proven.
Modernize an iconic physical product without preserving obsolete forms or betraying what users love.
